input.content:
# Multi-stage build for optimized Node.js application
# Stage 1: Builder
FROM node:20-alpine AS builder

WORKDIR /app

# Copy package files
COPY package*.json ./

# Install dependencies with npm ci for reproducible builds
RUN npm ci --production

# Copy application source
COPY src/ ./src/

# Stage 2: Runtime
FROM node:20-alpine

# Set working directory
WORKDIR /app

# Create non-root user for security
RUN addgroup -g 1001 -S nodejs && \
    adduser -S nodejs -u 1001

# Copy only necessary files from builder stage
COPY --from=builder --chown=nodejs:nodejs /app .

# Expose port
EXPOSE 3000

# Switch to non-root user
USER nodejs

# Health check
HEALTHCHECK --interval=30s --timeout=3s --start-period=5s --retries=3 \
    CMD node -e "require('http').get('http://localhost:3000', (r) => {if (r.statusCode !== 200) throw new Error(r.statusCode)})"

# Start application
CMD ["node", "src/index.js"]

version: '3.8'

services:
  app:
    build:
      context: .
      dockerfile: Dockerfile
    container_name: myproject-app
    ports:
      - "3000:3000"
    environment:
      - NODE_ENV=production
    restart: unless-stopped
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "node", "-e", "require('http').get('http://localhost:3000', (r) => {if (r.statusCode !== 200) throw new Error(r.statusCode)})"]
      interval: 30s
      timeout: 3s
      retries: 3
      start_period: 5s

# Docker configuration - Best practices reference file
# This file specifies which files/directories to exclude from Docker context
# to reduce image size and build time.

# Dependencies

#!/bin/bash

# Docker Build Script for Node.js Application
# This script provides utilities for building and managing Docker images

set -e

# Color codes for output
RED='\033[0;31m'
GREEN='\033[0;32m'
YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
BLUE='\033[0;34m'
NC='\033[0m' # No Color

# Configuration
APP_NAME="myproject"
REGISTRY="${DOCKER_REGISTRY:-docker.io}"
IMAGE_TAG="${IMAGE_TAG:-latest}"
BUILD_DATE=$(date -u +'%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ')
GIT_COMMIT=$(git rev-parse --short HEAD 2>/dev/null || echo "unknown")

# Print colored output
print_status() {
    echo -e "${BLUE}▶ $1${NC}"
}

print_success() {
    echo -e "${GREEN}✓ $1${NC}"
}

print_error() {
    echo -e "${RED}✗ $1${NC}"
}

print_warning() {
    echo -e "${YELLOW}⚠ $1${NC}"
}

# Show usage
usage() {
    cat << EOF
Usage: $0 [COMMAND] [OPTIONS]

Commands:
    build           Build Docker image
    build-no-cache  Build Docker image without cache
    push            Push image to registry
    run             Run container locally
    stop            Stop running container
    clean           Remove image and container
    help            Show this help message

Options:
    --tag TAG       Set image tag (default: latest)
    --registry URL  Set Docker registry (default: docker.io)
    --name NAME     Set application name (default: myproject)

Examples:
    $0 build
    $0 build --tag v1.0.0
    $0 build --registry ghcr.io --tag v1.0.0
    $0 run
    $0 clean

EOF
    exit 0
}

# Build Docker image
build_image() {
    print_status "Building Docker image: ${APP_NAME}:${IMAGE_TAG}"
    
    if ! docker build \
        --tag "${APP_NAME}:${IMAGE_TAG}" \
        --tag "${APP_NAME}:latest" \
        --build-arg BUILD_DATE="${BUILD_DATE}" \
        --build-arg GIT_COMMIT="${GIT_COMMIT}" \
        --label "org.label-schema.build-date=${BUILD_DATE}" \
        --label "org.label-schema.vcs-ref=${GIT_COMMIT}" \
        --label "org.label-schema.version=${IMAGE_TAG}" \
        . ; then
        print_error "Docker build failed"
        exit 1
    fi
    
    print_success "Docker image built successfully"
    docker images | grep "${APP_NAME}"
}

# Build image without cache
build_no_cache() {
    print_status "Building Docker image without cache: ${APP_NAME}:${IMAGE_TAG}"
    
    if ! docker build \
        --no-cache \
        --tag "${APP_NAME}:${IMAGE_TAG}" \
        --tag "${APP_NAME}:latest" \
        --build-arg BUILD_DATE="${BUILD_DATE}" \
        --build-arg GIT_COMMIT="${GIT_COMMIT}" \
        . ; then
        print_error "Docker build failed"
        exit 1
    fi
    
    print_success "Docker image built successfully (no cache)"
}

# Push image to registry
push_image() {
    print_status "Pushing image to registry: ${REGISTRY}/${APP_NAME}:${IMAGE_TAG}"
    
    # Tag with registry
    docker tag "${APP_NAME}:${IMAGE_TAG}" "${REGISTRY}/${APP_NAME}:${IMAGE_TAG}"
    docker tag "${APP_NAME}:latest" "${REGISTRY}/${APP_NAME}:latest"
    
    if ! docker push "${REGISTRY}/${APP_NAME}:${IMAGE_TAG}"; then
        print_error "Failed to push image"
        exit 1
    fi
    
    if ! docker push "${REGISTRY}/${APP_NAME}:latest"; then
        print_error "Failed to push latest tag"
        exit 1
    fi
    
    print_success "Image pushed successfully"
}

# Run container locally
run_container() {
    local container_id="${APP_NAME}-dev"
    
    # Stop existing container if running
    if docker ps -a --format '{{.Names}}' | grep -q "^${container_id}$"; then
        print_warning "Stopping existing container: ${container_id}"
        docker stop "${container_id}" 2>/dev/null || true
        docker rm "${container_id}" 2>/dev/null || true
    fi
    
    print_status "Running container: ${container_id}"
    
    docker run \
        --name "${container_id}" \
        --detach \
        --publish 3000:3000 \
        --env NODE_ENV=development \
        "${APP_NAME}:${IMAGE_TAG}"
    
    print_success "Container started successfully"
    sleep 2
    
    print_status "Container logs:"
    docker logs "${container_id}"
    
    echo ""
    print_success "Application running at http://localhost:3000"
}

# Stop running container
stop_container() {
    local container_id="${APP_NAME}-dev"
    
    if docker ps -a --format '{{.Names}}' | grep -q "^${container_id}$"; then
        print_status "Stopping container: ${container_id}"
        docker stop "${container_id}"
        docker rm "${container_id}"
        print_success "Container stopped"
    else
        print_warning "Container ${container_id} is not running"
    fi
}

# Clean up image and container
clean() {
    print_status "Cleaning up Docker resources..."
    
    # Stop and remove container
    local container_id="${APP_NAME}-dev"
    if docker ps -a --format '{{.Names}}' | grep -q "^${container_id}$"; then
        print_status "Removing container: ${container_id}"
        docker stop "${container_id}" 2>/dev/null || true
        docker rm "${container_id}" 2>/dev/null || true
    fi
    
    # Remove images
    if docker images --format '{{.Repository}}:{{.Tag}}' | grep -q "^${APP_NAME}:"; then
        print_status "Removing images: ${APP_NAME}"
        docker rmi "${APP_NAME}:${IMAGE_TAG}" 2>/dev/null || true
        docker rmi "${APP_NAME}:latest" 2>/dev/null || true
    fi
    
    print_success "Cleanup completed"
}

# Parse arguments
case "${1:-build}" in
    build)
        build_image
        ;;
    build-no-cache)
        build_no_cache
        ;;
    push)
        push_image
        ;;
    run)
        run_container
        ;;
    stop)
        stop_container
        ;;
    clean)
        clean
        ;;
    help|--help|-h)
        usage
        ;;
    *)
        print_error "Unknown command: $1"
        usage
        ;;
esac
